summary: Each photon of light carries an energy fixed by its frequency: E = h·f. Higher-frequency light means more energetic photons.
method: Identify the light's frequency (or convert from wavelength with f = c/λ).
Decide which quantity is unknown — energy or frequency.
Rearrange E = h·f for the unknown.
Use SI units (Hz, J); the calculator supplies h ≈ 6.63 × 10⁻³⁴ J·s.
Substitute and compute; results are tiny, so expect powers of ten like 10⁻¹⁹ J.

example.title: Energy of a green-light photon
steps: The unknown is E, so use E = h·f.
